items.title: CISA Flags Actively Exploited LiteSpeed cPanel Plugin Flaw (CVE-2026-54420)
items.summary: A CVSS 8.5 privilege escalation flaw in the LiteSpeed cPanel user-end plugin (versions before 2.4.8) lets attackers with FTP or web shell access abuse symbolic-link handling on CloudLinux/CageFS shared hosting to gain root. CISA added it to the KEV catalog on June 16, 2026, confirming active exploitation.
items.why: Shared hosting providers run this plugin at massive scale, so a root-level escalation bug under active attack puts a huge swath of web hosting infrastructure at risk of full server takeover.

items.title: DragonForce Ransomware Hides C2 Inside Microsoft Teams Relays via 'Backdoor.Turn'
items.summary: Symantec discovered Backdoor.Turn, a Go-based RAT that abuses Microsoft Teams' TURN relay infrastructure to mask command-and-control traffic as legitimate Teams network activity. DragonForce operators used it to hide inside a U.S. services firm for up to two months, performing credential theft, AD reconnaissance, and network scanning.
items.why: This is the first known malware to weaponize Teams' own relay servers for C2, meaning defenders watching for 'connections to Microsoft' as a trust signal need to rethink that assumption entirely.

items.title: Velvet Ant's 'Operation Highland' Hid Inside an Air-Gapped Network for Nearly a Decade
items.summary: Sygnia's forensic investigation found the China-nexus actor Velvet Ant maintained undetected access to an isolated internal network from 2016 onward by deploying nine distinct backdoored pam_unix.so variants that accepted hardcoded passwords and harvested credentials, plus a custom SOCKS5 proxy to reach systems beyond the initial foothold.
items.why: It proves air-gapping alone is not a security boundary if the authentication layer itself is compromised, and the scale of build infrastructure behind nine PAM variants shows a serious, long-term state-backed operation.

items.title: China-Linked SprySOCKS Backdoor Expands to Windows With Kernel-Driver Stealth
items.summary: ESET researchers documented two previously unknown Windows variants (WIN_DRV and WIN_PLUS) of the SprySOCKS backdoor, formerly believed Linux-only. WIN_DRV uses a kernel driver to intercept system calls and hide malicious processes/files. ESET attributes the tooling with high confidence to Earth Lusca (aka FishMonger), linked to Chinese contractor I-SOON, with use against government targets in Taiwan, Thailand, Pakistan, and Honduras.
items.why: A kernel-driver rootkit component bolted onto an established cross-platform backdoor significantly raises detection difficulty for one of the most active China-linked espionage toolsets.

items.title: PoC Released for Windows Snipping Tool NTLM Hash Leak (CVE-2026-33829)
items.summary: Black Arrow Security published a working PoC showing the Snipping Tool's ms-screensketch deep-link handler can be coerced into opening a UNC path on an attacker-controlled SMB server, silently leaking the victim's Net-NTLM hash while the tool opens normally on screen. Microsoft patched it in April 2026 by removing the vulnerable deep-link behavior.
items.why: The exploit needs nothing more than a single clicked link and produces no visible anomaly, making it an easy, low-friction credential-theft primitive for phishing-driven internal network attacks.
